rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to ports or features visible at that level.
Use annex agree blocks for component type contracts, and implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
generic_rules: For range constraints, use visible feature names and concrete bounds in type scope contracts.
Ensure AGREE expressions match the actual AADL data types of ports and variables.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not encode AADL properties like timing or unit tokens as AGREE variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must align with the actual data types of the referenced features.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean domain values into integers unless the port is explicitly typed as bool.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing-related AADL tokens
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent paths in type scopes
